audit_method: direct URL fetch — strip-first repair attempt, 2026-06-12. Each source URL fetched directly with curl or PDF text extraction; no WebSearch snippets used. Stopping rule applied because more than 80% of cite-pairs were partial or inaccessible after direct fetch.

rows.supporting_quote: Garfield County has transferred nearly $4.1 million in private activity bonds ( PABs ) to the Colorado Housing and Finance Authority ( CHFA ) to help bolster affordable housing initiatives in the area. ... Grosscup said the program has aided 298 individuals in acquiring a home since 2020. ... The board approved allocating its authority to issue the tax-exempt bonds to CHFA unanimously, 3-0.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl/PDF extraction. Supports PAB amount, CHFA transfer, homebuyer purpose, 298 residents since 2020, and unanimous vote.

rows.supporting_quote: Project Name: Aster Place ... this would be the first affordable low-income family apartment development to be built in Parachute, CO or the Battlement Mesa Special District. ... AP is zoned for the proposed 58 residential multifamily units ... The GCHA will be a Special Limited Partner
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl/PDF extraction. Supports Aster Place, first affordable family LIHTC framing, 58 units, and GCHA SLP role.

rows.supporting_quote: The 58-unit project, planned within the Battlement Mesa Planned Unit Development (PUD), would be the area’s first affordable housing development and may receive eight project-based vouchers through the Garfield County Housing Authority. ... Located on approximately 6.38 acres of vacant land on Five Stone Quarry Road in Parachute ... The Garfield County Housing Authority, which is also serving as the project’s Special Limited Partner, is expected to allocate eight project-based vouchers to Aster Place.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl/PDF extraction. Supports Aster Place acreage/location, affordability range context, GCHA SLP role, and eight projected PBVs.
